verse_specific_anchors: the analogical structure: childhood → youth → old age → another body, mapped to v1 → v2 → v3 → v4 of a single service
dhīra rendered with technical specificity (steady-minded, not 'wise')
tatra na muhyati ('is not bewildered regarding that') — preserved as 'whose nervous system does not flare at rewrite-time'
conformance test suite as the artifact analog of what holds across transitions
verdict_reason: Tested against three random verses from Chapters 4, 9, and 18: BG 4.7 (yadā yadā hi dharmasya — whenever dharma declines), BG 9.31 (kṣipraṁ bhavati dharmātmā — quickly becomes righteous), BG 18.61 (īśvaraḥ sarva-bhūtānāṁ — Lord seated in all hearts). The 2.13 engineering analog cannot equally apply to any of these. The state-transition-without-identity-collapse structure (v1 → v2 → v3 → v4) is uniquely a 2.13 reading; it depends on the analogical argument from kaumāram/yauvanam/jarā that the test verses lack. PASS.
